subject: [PATCHv7 09/18] mm/hugetlb: Defer vmemmap population for bootmem hugepages

reviews.author: Kiryl Shutsemau (author)
reviews.summary: Author reaffirmed their stance on keeping the SECTION_IS_VMEMMAP_PREINIT flag, citing its purpose in core-sparse and handling in hugetlb_vmemmap_init_late(), indicating no planned fix for the reviewer's concern.
reviews.raw_body:
I think keeping the flag is right thing to do.

SECTION_IS_VMEMMAP_PREINIT indicates to core-sparse that the section
should not be populated and it will be initialized elsewhere. Even in
!hugetlb_bootmem_page_zones_valid() we take care of it in
hugetlb_vmemmap_init_late().

And, as you mentioned, nobody looks at the flag after boot.

2026-03-17.patch_summary: This patch modifies the behavior of hugepage allocation during boot, deferring the population of vmemmap for bootmem hugepages until a later stage.
